calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-22-2010 Assigned To Trial Court

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Nov-22-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Prove-Up Hearing. Commissioner: Everett A. Hewlett, Jr., Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Lois Jones, CSR #3689.

Plaintiff Mary Lou Zaparolli was present in pro per, placed under oath and examined.
Edward Zaparollli, a witness for plaintiff, was placed under oath but did not testify.
Defendant Jose Alfredo Ortiz, dba William Towing, was present in pro per, placed under oath and examined.
Edward Wallace, a witness for defendant, was placed under oath but did not testify.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was filed on November 9, 2010.

Settlement agreement reached by the parties was placed on the record by plaintiff Mary Lou Zaparolli. According to the terms of the agreement, defendant Jose Alfredo Ortiz, dba William Towing, shall vacate the subject premises by December 15, 2010. On February 1, 2011, defendant Jose Alfredo Ortiz, dba William Towing, shall pay plaintiff Mary Lou Zaparolli the sum of $5,000.00. Commencing March 1, 2011, and continuing on the first of each month thereafter for the next five months, defendant Jose Alfred Ortiz shall pay plaintiff Mary Lou Zaparolli the sum of $1,000.00 each month. If the first of the month falls on a weekend or holiday, defendant's payment shall be due the next business day. Defendant shall have a three day grace period in which to cure any failure to adhere to the payment schedule.

If defendant Jose Alfredo Ortiz, dba William Towing, fails to adhere to the terms of the agreement set forth above, plaintiff Mary Lou Zaparolli is entitled to judgment in the amount of $24,500.00 less any payments made.

Parties were voir dired by the Court as to the terms and their understanding of the terms of the settlement agreement.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER
Off Calendar Nov-03-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - MARY LOU ZAPAROLLI, TEL. NO. 850-4033, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - NOTICE WAS NOT GIVEN TO THE OTHER PARTY AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF MARY LOU ZAPAROLLI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO PROVE THE ELEMENTS OF PLAINTIFF'S CASE WITH ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
